args.query: site:pge.com Medical Baseline Program disconnection notice health-related support PG&E California

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Medical Baseline Program

  - url: https://www.pge.com/en/account/billing-and-assistance/financial-assistance/medical-baseline-program.html

  - source: Pacific Gas and Electric Company

  - domain: pge.com


  ## Content


  # Medical Baseline Program


  PG&E''s Medical Baseline Program, also called Medical Baseline Allowance, supports
  residential customers who depend on power for certain medical needs.


  ## Program overview


  The program offers two main forms of assistance.


  First, eligible customers may receive an additional monthly allotment of energy
  at the lowest price available on their current tiered rate, or a Medical Baseline
  discount on certain eligible rates. The amount of additional electricity or gas
  depends on the household''s energy needs and is verified during medical certification.


  Second, enrolled customers receive additional notifications before a Public Safety
  Power Shutoff. PG&E generally aims to send PSPS notices in advance and again close
  to a shutoff. Medical Baseline customers may receive added calls, texts, emails
  or other contact attempts based on their account preferences.


  Enrollment in Medical Baseline does not remove the requirement to keep paying the
  regular PG&E bill. Utility service can still be disconnected for nonpayment.


  ## Eligibility


  Eligibility is based on medical conditions or medical needs rather than household
  income.


  To qualify, a full-time resident of the home must either have a qualifying medical
  condition or need a qualifying medical device to treat an ongoing medical condition.


  The official eligibility information gives examples of conditions involving serious
  illness, immune-system concerns, heating or cooling needs connected to a medical
  condition, and other listed medical needs. It also lists qualifying home-use medical
  devices. Examples include certain breathing equipment, oxygen equipment, dialysis
  equipment, infusion equipment, monitoring devices, mobility equipment and other
  devices used for ongoing medical care.


  The official list is not limited to one diagnosis or one device. Qualifying devices
  may include equipment used to sustain life and certain mobility equipment when supported
  by a licensed medical practitioner. The page also notes that some ordinary comfort
  or therapy devices do not qualify.


  Only one Medical Baseline application is required for a household.


  ## Applying or recertifying online


  The online process separates the customer''s application step from the medical practitioner''s
  certification step.


  1. The customer fills out and submits the online application.

  2. PG&E sends a confirmation number and instructions for the medical practitioner.

  3. The customer shares that confirmation number and the instructions with the practitioner.

  4. The practitioner completes the certification portion.

  5. After the practitioner confirms eligibility, the customer can be enrolled.


  ## Applying by form


  PG&E also provides a printable application and recertification form.


  For a paper or emailed application, the customer completes the customer portion
  and a doctor completes and signs the practitioner portion. PG&E states that a completed
  application must be submitted within 90 days of the medical practitioner''s signature.


  Completed forms can be mailed to the PG&E Billing Center Medical Baseline program
  in Stockton. PG&E also lists an email submission option for completed and certified
  application forms.


  These application and certification steps occur as part of formal enrollment. The
  program page distinguishes the general medical-needs eligibility framework from
  the later practitioner verification process used to approve an application.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Medical Baseline Program
url: https://www.pge.com/en/account/billing-and-assistance/financial-assistance/medical-baseline-program.html

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Self-Identified Vulnerable Program

  - url: https://www.pge.com/en/account/billing-and-assistance/financial-assistance/vulnerable-customer-status.html

  - source: Pacific Gas and Electric Company

  - domain: pge.com


  ## Content


  # Self-Identified Vulnerable Program


  PG&E offers the Self-Identified Vulnerable Program (SIV) for customers whose health
  or safety could be at risk during an outage and who do not qualify for the Medical
  Baseline Program. Enrollment adds a vulnerable-customer status to the account for
  one year, and the status can be extended when it expires.


  ## Program support


  Customers enrolled in SIV may receive additional support before and during certain
  service interruptions. The program includes proactive outreach during Public Safety
  Power Shutoffs, major emergencies and outages that last multiple days. If a customer
  does not respond to earlier PSPS notifications, PG&E may make an additional attempt
  through an in-person doorbell ring or a doorhanger.


  SIV customers may also receive extra notice before service is turned off for nonpayment.
  The program can provide a higher level of support when customers are looking for
  rebates, discounts or other assistance programs.


  The program is designed around a customer''s need for added communication and support.
  It is not necessary to provide a detailed personal history merely to set the initial
  SIV status.


  ## How to enroll


  Customers can self-enroll through PG&E My Account.


  1. From the account dashboard, go to Account settings and choose the option to update
  personal information.

  2. Open the Self-Identified Vulnerable section.

  3. Select the option to set SIV status.


  After these steps, the customer is enrolled and the SIV status takes effect.


  PG&E also offers an optional step where customers can describe circumstances that
  may help the company provide more tailored support. The optional categories cover
  broad situations such as age-related needs, sensory or mobility access needs, disability,
  use of durable medical equipment or assistive technology, or none of the listed
  categories.


  Providing those optional details is not required to remain in the program. Leaving
  the optional description blank does not disqualify the customer from SIV.


  ## Duration and assistance


  Once set, SIV status remains on the account for one year. Customers can request
  an extension when the status expires.


  Customers who need help with enrollment can contact PG&E customer support at 1-800-743-5000.
  PG&E also provides printable enrollment instructions.


  The SIV program does not replace ordinary outage reporting, billing obligations
  or other program-specific eligibility processes. Its purpose is to add support and
  communication for customers whose health or safety could be at risk during an interruption.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Self-Identified Vulnerable Program
url: https://www.pge.com/en/account/billing-and-assistance/financial-assistance/vulnerable-customer-status.html

I checked your PG&E account context and existing enrollments:

- **Medical Baseline:** Not enrolled  
- **Self-Identified Vulnerable (SIV) status:** Not enrolled  
- **Account:** Active residential electric service with a **pending disconnection notice**
- **Alerts on file:** Email and text

Since you’re handling payment separately, the health-related options are:

### 1. Medical Baseline — if medical equipment or a qualifying condition requires electricity
This may apply if someone who lives in the home uses qualifying ongoing medical equipment—such as oxygen, breathing, dialysis, infusion, monitoring, or certain mobility equipment—or has a qualifying medical condition requiring electricity.

It can provide:
- An additional monthly energy allowance or applicable discount
- Additional notifications for certain planned outages, including PSPS events

A licensed medical practitioner must certify the application. **Medical Baseline does not prevent disconnection for nonpayment.**

Apply through PG&E’s [Medical Baseline Program](https://www.pge.com/en/account/billing-and-assistance/financial-assistance/medical-baseline-program.html).

### 2. Self-Identified Vulnerable (SIV) status — if an interruption could put someone’s health or safety at risk
This may be the better immediate fit if you need extra communication and support but aren’t sure whether the situation meets Medical Baseline criteria. It does not require you to provide a detailed medical history just to set the status.

SIV can provide:
- Additional outreach during major outages, emergencies, and PSPS events
- Potentially extra notice before service is turned off for nonpayment
- The option to identify broad support needs, such as mobility, sensory, disability, or medical-equipment concerns

SIV lasts one year and can be self-enrolled in **PG&E My Account → Account settings → Personal information → Self-Identified Vulnerable**. Details are on PG&E’s [SIV page](https://www.pge.com/en/account/billing-and-assistance/financial-assistance/vulnerable-customer-status.html).

### What appears most relevant
If the household health need involves **qualifying medical equipment or a qualifying condition**, pursue **Medical Baseline**. If you primarily need PG&E to recognize that an outage or shutoff could create a health or safety risk—and you want added outreach—enroll in **SIV**, even if Medical Baseline is uncertain. You may want to pursue both if both descriptions fit.

For help enrolling, PG&E lists **1-800-743-5000**. These programs provide support and notifications but do not substitute for resolving the pending disconnection notice.
